verdict.answer_first: A states the problem immediately ('This snippet has a few real problems'), while B opens with boilerplate ('Reviewing this snippet directly...').
verdict.decisiveness: A provides a single, complete line of code (`Math.max(1, parseInt(...) || 1)`), while B's suggestion (`Math.min(rawPage, MAX_PAGE)`) is incomplete because it introduces an undefined variable.
verdict.fabrication: B invents a new requirement and variable, `MAX_PAGE`, which was not mentioned or implied in the original code.
verdict.actionability: A's code is a single, copy-pasteable line that works immediately. B's code requires me to stop and define `MAX_PAGE`.
